observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee who shall prepare Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) which contains the specific assignments/duties of the committee members, the cut-off date, and schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start to finish.

Thereafter, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Inventory Committee, GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to undertake the procedural guidelines for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to come up with more reliable account balances to be presented in the financial statements.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to prepare Property Cards and PPELC, respectively, for each class of PPE as required in MNGAS for LGUs.

observations.managementAction:

Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to restate the financial statements for CYs 2024 and 2023, to include the asset, liability, income and expense account balances of the SDC, to ensure accurate and fair presentation of the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to:

Ensure that all budget appropriations are consistently recorded in accordance with the classification prescribed in the approved appropriation ordinances;
Recognize and record obligations based on the total contract amounts upon contract award, including retention fees and future liabilities; and
Reconcile monthly the RAAOs and SAAOB.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to restate the CY 2024 SCBAA and revise the Reconciliation between Actual Amounts on a Comparable Basis as presented in the SCBAA and SFPerf, incorporating thereof the noted discrepancies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain RAAOs for all funds and budget items as mandated under the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Municipal Accountant and MBO to:

Conduct systematic monthly reconciliation of the RAAOs and SAAOB to promptly identify and resolve discrepancies; and
Trace and analyze the unreconciled balances amounting to ₱492,112.13 by reviewing the working schedules, accounting records, and budget registries to ensure the accuracy of any savings to be declared from CY 2024.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reclassify the Loans Payable-Domestic account into current and non-current by referring to the amortization schedules provided by the bank and other relevant financial records, if any; and
Ensure the inclusion of all relevant loan-related information in the NFS to enable users to make well-informed financial decisions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Make the necessary correcting entries to properly reflect the Trust Liability balances in the appropriate funds, as follows:
Reconcile the ₱46,645.24 discrepancy by validating the ₱17,341.56 variance in the beginning balance and the ₱29,303.68 difference in utilization, and make the necessary adjusting entries or revise the NFS; and
Conduct regular reconciliation to ensure accurate and reliable reporting of DRRMF balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Local Finance Committee and concerned offices to conduct a thorough review and validation of the local budget preparation forms prior to submission to the Local Sanggunian;
Require the MBO to strictly ensure that all appropriations and obligations in the budget registries are assigned with the correct account codes and classifications, aligned with the Annual/Supplemental Budgets and COA-prescribed Chart of Accounts; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and MBO to conduct regular reconciliation of budget and accounting records to promptly detect and correct any discrepancies prior to the preparation of monthly and year-end financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the HoPE, direct the:
BAC to:

Conduct an assessment of the workload and staffing capacity of the BAC Secretariat and Technical Working Group (TWG), and recommend additional personnel particularly as TWG member;
Prepare and implement a task distribution plan to allocate responsibilities evenly among BAC members, in order to manage the high volume of procurement activities and ensure that all procurement documents are reviewed and processed within the prescribed timeframes; and
Submit to the Audit Team copies of the following deficient procurement documents:

Minutes of the pre-procurement conference for the Construction of Flood Control at Paagan East;

1.21 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to (i) revert the equivalent balance of the
unreleased checks as of year-end to both the Cash in Bank and the
corresponding liability accounts; and (ii) ensure that any discrepancies
identified in the bank reconciliation statements are promptly investigated
and adjusted in the books, if warranted, to maintain the accuracy and
reliability of financial records; and
b. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to establish a robust
reconciliation procedure between their offices to promptly identify and
resolve discrepancies.

3.35 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the:
a) Municipal Engineer, being the building official, to inform all applicants of
building permits of their responsibilities after the completion of the
structure, which include, among others, the filing of a sworn declaration
of the true value of the structure. Likewise, inspect properties applied for
building permits that have not yet been applied for an occupancy permit
and compel the owner to perform their duties mandated by the law;
b) Municipal Assessor to verify the status of undeclared property and
henceforth declare the same in the name of the defaulting owner, if known,
or against an unknown owner, as the case may be, and assess the property
for taxation in accordance with laws, rules and regulations;
c) Municipal Treasurer to (i) regularly prepare and issue the necessary notice
of delinquency and demand letters to delinquent taxpayers; and (ii) post
the notice of the delinquency at the main entrance hall and in a publicly
accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay of the municipality
where the property is located; and
d) Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to recognize the
correcting entries on the understated property tax receivable accounts.

4.41 We reiterated and management agreed to take the following courses of action:
a. The Municipal Treasurer
i. Prioritize the finalization of the stratified schedule of presumptive
income level based on industry classifications, business size, location,
and other relevant operational indicators to approximate the gross
receipts of each business classification for every tax period. Once
established, it may serve as a standard reference in estimating gross
sales or receipts for businesses that fail to provide sufficient
documentation.
ii. Require all persons, who are granted a permit to conduct an activity or
business and who are liable to pay the business tax to submit a certified
photocopy of their income tax returns (ITR) on or before April 30 of
each year and collect any tax deficiency, if any, including interest at the
rate of ten percent (10%) corresponding to the two percent (2%) per
month from January to May. Otherwise, enforce the corresponding
penalty for non-compliance therewith;
iii. Conduct a thorough investigation into the underlying causes of the
decline in the number of business permits and license renewals,
including potential cases of unreported business closures and enjoin
business establishments to settle local taxes, fees, and charges prior to
the formal retirement or closure of their operations;
iv. Collect the tax deficiency from the concerned business entities; and
v. Enforce other remedies to ensure that the correct amount of fees and
taxes are collected.
b. The Business Permit and Licensing Officer ensure that applicants (i)
submit a complete set of all documentary requirements in securing
permits and licenses prior to the approval of the Local Chief Executive;
and (ii) declare the correct amount of gross sales in their application
consistent with the provisions of the Revenue Ordinance.

5.24 We recommended and management agreed to:
a. direct the accountable officers to ensure that all cash advances granted are
fully liquidated on or before year-end; otherwise, any unliquidated
balances should be promptly refunded and processed in the ensuing year
in accordance with applicable regulations;
b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the policy of not
issuing additional cash advances unless all previous advances have been
fully liquidated and settled, as required by existing rules and regulations;
and
c. ensure that the cash accountability of any accountable officer does not
exceed the maximum amount covered by their approved fidelity bond;
otherwise, increase the fidelity bond coverage, as warranted, ensuring that
the bond amount is proportionate to their respective accountabilities.

6.34 We recommended and the Management agreed to submit the Certification of
Savings with Sanggunian Resolution covering the reversion of savings and henceforth
comply with the documentary requirement thereon.
6.35 Furthermore, we recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to
instruct the:
a) Municipal Development Council to ensure that PPAs included in the AIP
and funded under the 20% DF are implementation-ready, considering
procurement timelines and implementation windows clearly aligned with
the fiscal calendar;
b) Municipal Engineer to (i) collaborate with relevant departments and
agencies to ensure the timely completion of projects; and (ii) prepare
variation order should a change of work from the original plan occur;
c) Chairman and Members of the BAC to (i) invite all necessary observers in
different stages of procurement; and (ii) post all NOAs, NTPs and
approved contracts to PhilGEPS; and
d) Municipal Accountant to (i) coordinate with the BAC and submit all the
documentary requirements, if warranted, otherwise submit an explanation
for non-submission thereof; and (ii) verify all supporting documents as to
the completeness and ensure that the same formed part of the supporting
documents to the disbursement vouchers before effecting payments
thereof.

7.20 We recommended and the management agreed to (a) prioritize the filling of the
mandatory position required by R.A. No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No.
2014-1 for the effective, efficient and continued discharge of the duties and responsibilities
of the LDRRMO; (b) instruct the concerned personnel to prepare, maintain and submit the
required registries and other reportorial requirements and disclosures pertinent to the
utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund including in-kind aids/donations;
and (c) direct the BAC to adhere consistently with the processes and documentary
requirements stated in the IRR of R.A. No. 9184

8.19 We recommended and the management agreed to (i) ensure the existence of
MOA with the source agency for every fund transfer to formalize its responsibilities
and obligations; and (ii) exert utmost effort to facilitate the prompt approval of the
necessary authorizing documents to avoid delays in the implementation of programs,
projects, and activities funded through inter-agency transfers
8.19 Likewise, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance with the reportorial requirements of
each trust fund to maintain transparency and accountability in the management of
financial resources sourced externally.

9.11 We reiterated our recommendation and management agreed to initiate strict
control measures in reviewing the supporting documents of the payments of salaries
to job order personnel.

10.25 We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive, thru the
MENRO-designate and his subordinates, agreed to:
(a) enjoin all barangays to support the solid waste management program of
the municipality through continued efforts to educate their respective
constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials
from compostable wastes; strict enforcement of “No Segregation, No
Collection Policy”; and establishment of functional MRF in all barangays
or clustered MRF among barangays; and
(b) (i) stop accepting and accommodating wastes not considered residual from
the barangays; and (ii) strengthen the implementation of existing
ordinances and policies on solid waste management especially on the
violators of open burning.
10.26 Likewise, we recommended that collectors and other personnel directly
dealing with the collection of solid waste be regularly equipped with personal
protective equipment and proper training to protect them from the hazards of
handling solid waste.

11.9 We recommended and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer,
together with the GAD Focal Person and TWG, agreed to prioritize the establishment
of a GAD database containing gender-disaggregated data across relevant sectors that
will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy
formulation.

the premiums and loan amortizations withheld and collected in the
previous month; and (ii) necessary journal entries to adjust an affected
account were timely made to ensure the accuracy of balances and prevent
significant ledger variances.

13.10 We recommended and the Management agreed to (i) ensure that the amount

withheld and collected in the previous month; (ii) discontinue the use of negative

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Provide entry for the recognition of “Transfers of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds” and Trust Liability-DRRM amounting to ₱2,436,420.12; and
Submit authority for the use of Trust Liability-DRRM amounting to ₱21,230.00 and explanation to the recognition of Trust Liability amounting to ₱3,260.81 during the year. If these were valid charges and recognition, these items shall be reflected in the NFS, otherwise, make adjusting entry to reflect the actual nature of the said transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Post entries for the recognition and derecognition of Advances to Contractors and CIP accounts, respectively, by the same amount of ₱892,004.51; and
In the ensuing years, follow the correct recognition of CIP and Advances to Contractors accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Accountant to provide adjusting entries for the derecognition and recognition of PPE and inventory accounts, respectively, in the amount of ₱2,027,922.50;
Municipal Accountant to restate the financial statements as a result of prior period adjustments; and
Inventory Committee to conduct inventory taking to support existence of the PPE items.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
request the SB to pass a resolution authorizing the transfer of multi-purpose buildings and other facilities to the various recipient barangays, and then prepare Deeds of Donation for the actual transfer and acceptance by the latter to substantiate the transfer of ownership of the said properties; and
secure ownership or Deeds of Donation for the construction/improvement made in private properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee who shall prepare PIP which contains the specific assignments/duties of the committee members, the cut-off date, and schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start to finish.
Thereafter, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Inventory Committee, GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to undertake the procedural guidelines for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to come up with more reliable account balances to be presented in the financial statements.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to prepare Property Cards and PPELC, respectively, for each class of PPE as required in MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit collections intact on a daily basis or on the next banking day in order to avoid the accumulation of huge balances which may result in possible loss or misappropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and/or BAC Chairman, to facilitate the immediate submission of the above identified lacking documents for audit purposes to ascertain the legality, validity and propriety of the paid transactions in adherence with the existing laws, rules and regulations; and
Municipal Accountant to meticulously scrutinize all DVs submitted for processing, ensuring that these are completely supported with the required documents before authorizing payments, thereby substantiating the legality, validity and propriety of the transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Engineer to provide for Detailed Estimates or DUPA for the lump-sum amount CSHP taking into consideration the minimum requirement for OSHS by the DOLE; 
Municipal Engineer to conduct inspection from time-to-time and assess the compliance of the contractor with standards and provide documentation for each inspection; and
Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to collect overpayment of at least ₱72,172.50 from the contractors.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to withhold taxes based on gross amount.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to provide the specific target dates of implementation of the PPAs identified in the AIP and target schedule for each activity in the AIP; and
The Municipal Budget Officer to:
use the savings from completed projects under the 20% DF and Share from Tobacco Excise Tax Fund (RA No. 7171 and 8240) and re-appropriate the same to new PPAs allowed under the said fund; and
seek the assistance of implementing offices like the Municipal Engineering Office to come up with a menu of new PPAs that will foster social and economic development, as well as, advance the self-reliance of tobacco farmers and promote the development of the tobacco industry.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct both the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to establish regular reconciliation procedures between their offices to promptly identify and resolve discrepancies, as part of the Municipality’s strong internal control system.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the        (i) Municipal Development Council to stop the practice of inclusion of generic items in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget, otherwise secure prior authority from the Local Sanggunian; (ii) Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that any change in the annual budget is supported with valid authority and documentation pursuant to Section 321 of R.A. No. 7160; and (iii) Municipal Engineer to prepare variation order should a change of work from the original plan occur.
Likewise, we recommended that Management work collectively towards ensuring the timely completion of projects, thus enabling the prompt realization of benefits for the intended beneficiaries

We commended Management’s effort to substantially comply with the previous year’s recommendations. However, we recommended that (a) the newly created positions be filled up for the effective and efficient discharge of the duties and responsibilities of the LDRRMO; (b) LDRRMC ensure that the entire unexpended LDRRMF of the previous years is included under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities appropriated therefrom; and (c) the concerned personnel prepare, maintain and submit the required registries and other reportorial requirements and disclosures pertinent to the utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund including in-kind aids/donations.

We recommended that management ensure to establish a MOA with the source agency for every fund transfer in order to formalize its responsibilities and obligations.
Likewise, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
Municipal Accountant and/or Treasurer to (i) ensure compliance with the posting and reportorial requirements of each trust fund to maintain transparency and accountability in the management of financial resources sourced externally; (ii) submit to the Audit Team the Relief Distribution Sheets in relation to the funds from the CGD; and (iii) make sure that all payments are accompanied with complete supporting documents; and
MSWDO to (i) clearly specify the type of financial assistance granted to each beneficiary; and (ii) ensure that all necessary documentary requirements for the grant of AICS, as provided in the DSWD MC No. 16, series of 2022, are completed and submitted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
Chairman and Members of the BAC and TWG to (i) invite all necessary observers in different stages of procurement; (ii) carefully scrutinize the documentary requirements submitted by the bidder; (iii) post all NOAs, NTPs and approved contracts to PhilGEPS; (iv) require COSH Program approved by DOLE for all infrastructure projects prior to perfection of contract; (v) ensure the submission by the winning bidder of a valid performance security prior to signing of the contract to safeguard the government from untoward act of the former, if there be any  (vi) diligently observe each procurement activity stated in the IRR of R.A. No. 9184; (vii) ensure that the contract duration specified in the Invitation to Bid (ITB), schedule submitted by the contractor, contract agreement, and other relevant documents are consistent and identical; and (viii) attend seminars and trainings related to government procurement to keep abreast of  pertinent provisions thereof; and
Municipal Accountant to (i) coordinate with the BAC and submit all the documentary requirements, if warranted, otherwise submit an explanation for non-submission thereof; (ii) apply the provisions governing the retention money; (iii) demand the posting of security before granting a 15% advance payment; and (iv) verify all supporting documents as to the completeness and ensure that the same formed part of the supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers before effecting payments thereof.

We reiterated our recommendations that management initiate the following actions:
refrain from hiring Job Order personnel whose functions overlap with those regular employees or are deemed recurring, necessary, and essential to the Municipality;
require the 33 JO personnel to appear at the COA office for  evaluation and verification; and
strictly implement control measures on the required submission of DTRs prior to the actual day of payout as the basis in the preparation of payrolls.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, thru the MENRO-designate and his subordinates:
enjoin all barangays to support the solid waste management program of the municipality through continued efforts to educate their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes, strict enforcement of “No Segregation, No Collection Policy”; and establishment of functional MRF in all barangays or clustered MRF among barangays; and
strengthen the implementation of existing ordinances and policies on solid waste management at the barangay level, especially the provision of adequate PPE and training to collectors and prohibition on burning of wastes, in order to achieve a community compliance and eventually abide by the provisions on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
Municipal Planning and Development Officer, together with the GAD Focal Person and TWG, to exert effort in establishing a GAD database that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation;
GAD Focal Point Person to (i) submit its HGDG assessment results for the GPB and Accomplishment Report; (ii) consistently coordinate with the MBO to ensure the accuracy of reports submitted to oversight agencies; (iii) communicate any changes in the GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG; and (iv) comply with the GAD mechanisms and processes to sustain the LGU’s critical consciousness in supporting gender and development, women’s empowerment and responding to gender issues pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01; and
MBO to confer with the GFPS and prepare and submit the report on obligations and projects to which the GAD programs were attributed.

We have further recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to locate documents and determine the details of unidentified land accounts for proper recognition and establishment of ownership thereon.
b. 11 Parcels of land identified by the Municipal Assessor as property of the LGU were not recorded in the books.
We have recommended that the Management:
1. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the General Services Officer and Municipal Assessor to exert further efforts in retrieval of documents required to establish ownership and cost valuation of the unrecorded land to facilitate its recording in the books. at acquisition cost, or fair value, if acquired through non-exchange transaction;
2. Make the necessary adjustment by recording the land account at the acquisition cost, or fair value, if acquired through non-exchange transaction, based on available supporting documents; and
3. Initiate the transfer of ownership of road lots to various barangays which are not held for use by the Municipal Government.
observations.managementAction: The management has already signed the MOPA with DENR in relation to the titling of lands owned by the LGU. The Municipality pursues the titling of all their lands by 2024, after the conduct of General Revision in 2023. The Municipality titled the site of the Sanitary Landfill and Memorial Park. The ownership of other parcels was secured though covered only with Tax Declarations. The transfer of ownership of road lots to respective barangays was not undertaken due to the limited resources of the barangays and difficulty of retrieving necessary supporting documents. Complete retrieval of documents/records of the subject properties cannot be fully facilitated.
observations.reason: Titling efforts are ongoing but not complete. Copies of required documents were not fully retrieved

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management to refrain from using the Petty Cash Fund for the payment of regular expenditures such as, internet expenses, office supplies, travelling and gas which could be paid out of other regular disbursement process.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed all accountable officers to strictly observe Section 6.1.5 of the Joint Circular and to only process and pay claims with complete documentation. The Local Chief Executive also instructed the submission of required quarterly report on utilization of confidential fund to the Secretary of DILG and to forward the Liquidation Report and supporting documents to the COA ICFAU. All petty expenses were instructed to be paid with complete documentation. However, some petty expenses were still being paid out of PCF contrary to the Circular. The OIC-Municipal Treasurer expressed to look into revision of internal policy.
observations.reason: PCF related issues persist. Some payments not allowed under PCF were still disbursed, replenishments reached high percentages and multiple times per month. The Municipality has not fully complied with requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Engineer to:
1. Request assistance of NMIS for re-evaluation in order to issue license to operate the said Slaughterhouse and consequently earning income to defray its operational expenses; and
2. Strictly comply to the policy, standards and guidelines set forth by the NMIS.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Provincial Veterinarian has conducted validation and advised the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist that the facility is registered as “Locally Registered Meat Establishment” for the time being while waiting for the NMIS registration. The Municipality will strictly comply to NMIS standards. NMIS registration was not yet secured and license was not yet issued.
observations.reason: NMIS registration was not yet secured

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management adopt measures that would facilitate the submission of their accounts on time as prescribed in the foregoing Sections of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and P.D. 1445. We have further recommended that additional staff with accounting background be assigned at the Accounting Office in order to address the delay on the submission of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement Vouchers, Official Receipts and other accounts reports were being submitted to the audit team though not always on time. Works in the Accounting Office were mostly undertaken by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Timely submission of accounts was not regularly complied with. For some months delay of 1-2 months was noted in the submission of Disbursement Vouchers, ORs and other reports. Additional staff with accounting background was not assigned.

observations.recommendation:

the:

a. OIC-Municipal Treasurer to declare all collections promptly to the
Municipal Accountant for timely recognition in the books;

b. Municipal Accountant to:

i. Restate the CY 2021 by recognizing the ₱870,039.86 advance
collection of RPT/SET and other collections in December 2021; and

ii. Record the ₱852,390.00 collections and its related accounts in CY

3.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to restate the CY 2021 financial statements based from the
entries provided by the audit team. The restatement should support
journal entries and schedules to support the recognition made by the
Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the LCE substantially comply with the Circular by:
a. Enjoining the concerned/responsible offices, first and foremost, to properly and adequately plan the retrieval of records, conduct of inventory and recording of LRNs;
b. Directing the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer-Designate on the retrieval of the documents related to LRNs constructed prior to CY2016; and
c. Instructing the Property Officer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the pertinent records and reports.
observations.managementAction: The LCE together with the concerned departments/ offices convened in an effort to address the issue. The concerned departments had not managed to work hand on due to workloads in their departments. No extensive report or record was prepared yet. Records and reports cannot be prepared due to absence of supporting schedules.
observations.reason: The LCE had directed concerned departments/ offices to facilitate the retrieval of records and the conduct of inventory. The LCE together with the concerned departments/ offices convened in an effort to address the issue.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Transfer the Trust-Fund-related accounts incorporated and lodged under the General Fund books to the Trust Fund books; and
b. Make sufficient disclosures with regard to the transactions, events, and conditions under the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: Identified accounts by the audit team were already recorded under the Trust Fund. LDRRMF-Special Trust and Performance Challenge Fund were already recorded under the Trust Fund.
observations.reason: Identified accounts KALAHI-CIDDS and KALAHI-BUM/AM, LDRRMF-Special Trust and Performance Challenge Fund were recorded under the Trust Fund. Nonetheless, this CY 2022, we noted that several transactions were recorded and maintained under the General Fund instead of Trust Fund. This includes, among others, receipts of bidding documents and “one COVID-19 Allowance” from DOH.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the:
a. Department/ Office Heads to:
1. Properly plan their PPAs taking into utmost consideration possible contingencies in the future to avoid revisions/ adjustments that were not considered in the AIP; and
2. Notify the appropriate offices/committees for their inclusion or incorporation in the AIP by crafting a supplemental AIP, or any other means prescribed by the Manual and the Code, if there are revisions/ adjustments.
b. Appropriate offices/committees, and reviewing bodies to:
1. Conduct strict and rigorous review of the local budget preparation forms, as to the contents and substance; and
2. Ensure the consistency of the budgets (annual and supplemental) with the AIPs (annual and supplemental) pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Manual and the Code, as discussed.
We further recommended that the LCE exercise utmost diligence and competence in its dealings and transactions, most importantly in the preparation of documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Office was closely coordinating with the Municipal Budget Office as regards plan to ensure that programmed PPAs were monitored. Proposed PPAs to be budgeted were strictly monitored by the departments and the reviewing bodies. Moreover, offices were instructed to prepare their budget forms after thorough review and notify the MPDO and MBO for any changes. The LCE committed that the same observation will not re-occur for subsequent audit years.
observations.reason: The departments and the reviewing bodies were already coordinating. The programming of PPAs under the Annual and Supplemental Budgets were in line with DBM-DBM-DOF-DILG guidance and were incorporated in AIP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that LCE, direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to:
a. promptly and directly deposit the SEF collections intact in the SEF account within the prescribed period; and
b. prepare separate reports of collections and cashbook for SEF collections.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Treasurer, after being informed, immediately recorded and deposited SEF collections directly to SEF account. Cashbook for SEF collections was maintained in a separate cashbook by the Municipal Treasurer. On a separate note, lapses were noted as regards the procedures in the recording of SEF collections in the books of the LGU, maintained by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Collections of SET were already reported and directly deposited to the SEF account. Cashbook for SEF collections was maintained by the OIC-Municipal Treasurer. However, issues on recording by the Municipal Accountant remain (see Observation No. 5, Part II).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:
a. Ensure that the PPAs to be programmed under the 20% DF are incorporated in the AIPs (annual and the AIPs supplemental);
b. Ascertain that the unutilized balances of MOOE-related PPAs appropriated under the 20% DF are reverted back to the General Fund, and if these reverted balances are intended to be utilized/ appropriated, enact a Supplemental Budget through an appropriation ordinance or law, as the case may be; and
c. Exercise utmost diligence and competence in its dealings and transactions, most importantly in the preparation of documents.
observations.managementAction: Management ensured the inclusion of PPAs identified under the 20% DF in the CY 2022 Annual and Supplemental Budgets. The PPAs under the 20% DF were in line with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and RA No. 7160. The PPAs programmed and executed in CY 2022 were implemented and supported by investment programs.
observations.reason: The Management ensured the inclusion of programmed PPAs in CY 2022 Annual and Supplemental Budgets and Investment Programs supporting the programmed and implemented PPAs for CY 2022.

observations.managementAction: For CY 2022, almost all PPAs identified in 2021, the Municipality now ensuring that the programmed projects were implemented. The Management is currently in the process of implementing the subject parcel of lands. The management has already signed the MOPA with DENR in relation to the titling of lands owned by the LGU. The Municipality now showcasing that projects were feasible under the 20% DF.
observations.reason: For CY 2022, almost all PPAs identified in 2021 were implemented and the Municipality ensured that the programmed PPAs identified under the 20% DF are feasible. However, transfer of parcel of land to Municipality was not immediately facilitated due to pandemic and documentary requirements not yet completed. Copies of Resolution and Memorandum of Agreement were not furnished to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the LCE direct the Local Development Council to avoid appropriating MOOE-related PPAs under the 20% Development Fund. Correspondingly, invest on infrastructure projects/equipment or any other capital expenditures in allocating its funds to cater all its constituents to enjoy social, economic and environmental development essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the people.
observations.managementAction: Strictly complied with. The 20% DF was programmed and utilized in nature of eligible developmental projects. The Municipality was guided with Item 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 in the programming, budgeting and utilization of the 20% DF.
observations.reason: The 20% DF PPAs programmed and utilized in CY 2022 were eligible developmental projects and do not fall under the enumerated expenditures not allowed to be charged against the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to strictly enforce the provisions cited in Section 272 of RA No 7160 and Section 86 of the Manual on NGAS and LGUs and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the close monitoring and recording of SET collections.
observations.managementAction: Collections of SET were already reported and deposited directly to the SEF account.
observations.reason: Collections of SET were already reported and directly deposited to the SEF account.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer, and other local officials to process and pay claims with complete documentation only.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed all accountable officers to strictly observe Section 6.1.5 of the Joint Circular and to only process and pay claims with complete documentation. The Local Chief Executive also instructed the submission of required quarterly report on utilization of confidential fund to the Secretary of DILG and to forward the Liquidation Report and supporting documents to the COA ICFAU. No Confidential Expenses were charged in CY 2022.
observations.reason: All accountable officers were instructed to comply and no confidential expenses were charged in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Management to require the certified copy of Annual Income Tax Returns to Business Taxpayers on the second quarter of the year and adjust the business tax and collect the difference if there are any discrepancies.
observations.managementAction: Requirement for Certified Copy of Annual Income Tax Return is being complied with as of date. Certified Copies of Annual Tax Returns were requested and compliance is being undertaken.
observations.reason: Requirement of certified copies of Annual Income Tax Returns is being complied with.

observations.managementAction: The LGU has limited Plantilla Position and cannot create new positions as of date because of the 55% PS limitation. The hiring of JOs was to address the need of the Municipality of additional manpower. Procedures on hiring of the JOs were complied with.
observations.reason: Procedures on hiring of the JOs were complied with. Plantilla limitations prevent creation of plantilla positions

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that immediate installation of adequate controls for requisition of gasoline and other oil derivatives must be made through enforcement of necessary documents as required by existing rules and regulations such as:
a. Corresponding driver’s trip tickets and monthly report of official travels must be attached in the Disbursement Vouchers for payment of gasoline and oil derivative;
b. The supply officer must prepare reconciliation statement between the detailed schedule of gasoline issued by the gasoline station / dealer and the supply officer’s record. Duplicate of requisition slip must be kept on file by the Supply Officer as one of his bases in preparing reconciliation statement; and
c. A written agreement or contract with the gasoline dealer must be undertaken to formalize the terms and conditions as well as consumption limitations/restrictions.
We have recommended further that these transactions/disbursements should be subjected to tax as mandated under Revenue Memorandum No. 23-2012 dated May 8, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Office instructed all users of the municipal service vehicles to secure Driver’s Trip Tickets to support use of vehicle and claims for payments of fuels. The GSO was advised to follow COA audit pertinent recommendations. The supply officer and Accounting Office were instructed to follow COA and BIR circulars. However, reconciliation of detailed schedule of issued gasoline was not facilitated due to non-preparation of detailed schedule. Written agreement with gasoline dealers is under negotiation. Withholding of taxes was strictly complied with since January 2020.
observations.reason: Driver’s Trip Tickets were used to support use of vehicles and claims for fuels. Withholding of taxes was being complied with since January 2020. However, reconciliation statements and written agreements with gasoline dealers were not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended further that the members of the BAC and BAC Secretariat especially coordinate with each other and ensure completeness of procurement documents to effectively facilitate submission to COA.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Office is now complying in the timely submission of Purchase Orders and Contracts and Disbursement Vouchers were being transmitted to the Office. The BAC was not able to submit all subject PPAs Purchase Orders on the same day Disbursement Vouchers were being submitted. There was still delay to justify compliance with the requirement due to distance between the municipality and the auditor’s office
observations.reason: Not Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit the revised Summary or a new Schedule/ Report specifying the exact day and the month of the actual recognition, and the estimated useful life of each item of PPE for the Audit Team’s validation. Submit also the necessary documents in support of the actual date of recognition;
Discontinue the policy of assigning residual value to the LRNs; and
After accomplishing the no. 1 recommendation and after the recomputation of the exact and correct Accumulated Depreciation as of December 31, 2020 and Depreciation Expense for CY2021 by taking into consideration the discussion herein, adjust the affected accounts in accordance with applicable provisions of the IPSAS using the recommended adjusting entry as follows:

We recommended that the LCE substantially comply with the Circular by:
Enjoining the concerned/responsible offices, first and foremost, to properly and adequately plan the retrieval of records, conduct of inventory and recording of LRNs;
Directing the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer-Designate on the retrieval of the documents related to LRNs constructed prior to CY2016; and

3.21 	The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer will closely coordinate to effect the reconciliation and retrieval of documents related to LRNs constructed prior to CY2016 and conduct inventory and record the said LRNs.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Transfer the Trust-Fund-related accounts incorporated and lodged under the General Fund books to the Trust Fund books; and
Make sufficient disclosures with regard to the transactions, events, and conditions under the Trust Fund.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
Department/ Office Heads to:
Properly plan their PPAs taking into consideration possible contingencies in the future to avoid revisions/ adjustments that were not considered in the AIP; and
Notify the appropriate offices/committees for their inclusion or incorporation in the AIP by crafting a supplemental AIP, or any other means prescribed by the Manual and the Code if there are revisions/ adjustments.
Appropriate offices/committees, and reviewing bodies to:
Conduct strict and rigorous review of the local budget preparation forms, as to the contents and substance; and
Ensure the consistency of the budgets (annual and supplemental) with the AIPs (annual and supplemental) pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Manual and the Code, as discussed.
5.30 	We further recommended that the LCE exercise utmost diligence and competence in its dealings and transactions, most importantly in the preparation of documents.
5.31    Management commented during exit conference that they favorably accepted the observation and agreed to comply with the audit recommendation. Further, they will resolve for the next and incoming budget years in linking the Annual Budget to harmonize plans and policies by meticulously preparing the required LBP forms and the proposed amount per expense class be specified in separate form. They admitted that the excluded PPAs in CY 2021 AIP were already executed or implemented.

We recommended that LCE, direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to:
promptly and directly deposit the SEF collections intact in the SEF account within the prescribed period; and
prepare separate reports of collections and cashbook for SEF collections.
During exit conference, the OIC-Municipal Treasurer adhered and committed to comply with the audit recommendation by maintaining separate cashbook and separate reports of collections.

We recommended that the LCE:
Ensure that the PPAs to be programmed under the 20% DF are incorporated in the AIPs (annual and supplemental);
Ascertain that the unutilized balances of MOOE-related PPAs appropriated under the 20% DF are reverted back to the General Fund, and if these reverted balances are intended to be utilized/ appropriated, enact a Supplemental Budget through an appropriation ordinance or law, as the case may be; and
Exercise utmost diligence and competence in its dealings and transactions, most importantly in the preparation of documents.
During the exit conference, management commented that the project “Rehabilitation/Construction of Irrigation System and Construction of Poblacion Irrigation System” is included in their 2021 Annual Investment Plan is one and the same project. The said project is implemented in observance with existing rules and regulations. It is in good faith that the error is typographical in nature, whereas the fund source indicated is RA 7171 instead of 20% Development fund.
We reiterated our recommendations that management exercise utmost diligence in the preparation of documents to ensure that PPAs budgeted under development fund are actually and accurately included in the AIP.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Assessor and MPDC to judiciously and rigorously plan, budget and evaluate the procurement of parcel of land under the 20% DF in order to avoid the occurrence of the foregoing circumstances, such as conducting a feasibility study, legal and technical consultations to ensure the viability of the intended infrastructure projects in the future.
We also recommended that the LCE:
Facilitate the transfer of the subject parcel of lands in the name of the municipality by executing a notarized deed of absolute sale or its equivalent, and other documentary requirements needed for the transfer of property;
Request the Sangguniang Bayan Members to authorize him to enter into Memorandum of Agreement with the developer;
Issue supplemental guidelines specifying the timelines in the execution of the projects to provide a sense of urgency among the parties; and
Execute a memorandum of agreement or written agreement between the LGU and the developer to determine both parties’ participation in the execution/implementation of the socialized housing.
During exit conference, Management commented that they are in the process of preparing documents necessary for the transfer of the said land under the name of the LGU. Moreover, they commit to abide with the audit recommendations.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS by adequately providing a concise narrative on significant changes and breakdown of material balances to ensure that relevant information is available to the intended users of the Financial Statements.
The Municipal Accountant favorably accepted the observation and agreed to comply with the audit team recommendation.

We have recommended that the LCE direct the:
Municipal Engineer to provide copies of CoC and completely signed SWA to the Municipal Accountant upon the completion of a certain project that would be her basis in recognizing the completed project in the books.
Municipal Accountant to:
adjust the CIP account and recognize the correct amount of the completed projects, reclassify the same to the appropriate PPE accounts and recognize corresponding depreciation; and
maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for each PPA for proper recording and monitoring;
Management favorably accepted the observation and agreed to comply with the audit recommendation and assured the Audit team that the Municipal Engineer will regularly furnish the Municipal Accountant the SWA and CoC of completed projects that would be her basis in recognizing as asset in the books. Further, the Municipal Accountant will draw the necessary adjustments for the fair presentation in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the LCE direct the following concerned heads of offices:
Municipal Engineer to:
a. Closely coordinate with barangays and Assessor’s Office in the monitoring of construction/improvement of buildings with no permit;
b. 	Furnish the Assessor’s Office copies of issued Building Permit and Certificate of Occupancy Permit, as well as complete building plans as reference and support in their inspection and appraisal; and
c. Monitor the issuance of building and occupancy permits.
Municipal Assessor to:
Conduct field inspection in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer and Bureau of Fire Protection; and
Comply with Section 6, Chapter IV of MRPAAO and require the Municipal Engineer copies of Certificate of Occupancy Permit issued as reference and support in the appraisal of buildings.
Management favorably accepted the audit observation and adhered to comply with the recommendation. Moreover, the Municipal Assessor ensure that necessary coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer and the Bureau of Fire Protection before the conduct of any field inspection. Management assured the Audit team regarding the procedures to be taken and had already created a team for Monitoring of Building Permits and Occupancy permits.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) institute and adopt a sound system of internal control in safeguarding the agency’s assets, particularly Cash through directing:
the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to follow-up the said SB Resolution and prepares all the necessary documents and transact to the Branch Manager of LBP regarding the intention to avail of the bank’s ATM facility, and
the Human Resources Management Officer (HRMO) to enroll employees for LBP automated Payroll Clearing System so that once the ATM is installed, the LGU can start using it and reduce the cash accountability of the DO which would eventually reduce or mitigate the risk of loss thru misuse, theft, robbery or misapplication of government funds.
We have further recommended that the LCE effect the increase of the fidelity bonds of the OIC-Municipal Treasurer commensurate to their accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.
The OIC-Municipal Treasurer commented that with regard to the issue of having large amount of cash advances due to manual distribution of salaries, her office has already requested the Sangguniang Bayan to draft and pass a resolution to make the municipal payroll through ATM. She further averred that this will eventually be at their advantage because their office will no longer entertain long queues and more tiring way of distributing payroll. On the issue of fidelity bond, the OIC-Municipal Treasurer commented that she will apply for a larger amount proportionate to that of the average cash she is handling and if possible, she manifested the approval and assistance as soon as the next payroll is at hand.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact and daily or on the next banking day.  We have further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of temporarily using the collection to pay petty expenses.
The OIC-Municipal Treasurer favorably accepted the observation and agreed to comply with the audit recommendation.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) through the BAC and TWG to conduct strict, extensive and efficient bid-evaluation and post-qualification by scrupulously scrutinizing the documents submitted by the bidder particularly the Statement of All On-going Government and Private Contracts, List of Contractor’s Personnel of contractors and List of Contractor’s Equipment.
Management favorably accepted the observation and adhered to comply with the audit recommendation.

We have recommended that the LCE direct the Local Development Council to avoid appropriating MOOE-related PPAs under the 20% Development Fund. Correspondingly, invest on infrastructure projects/equipment or any other capital expenditures in allocating its funds to cater all its constituents to enjoy social, economic and environmental development essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the people.
Management accepted the observation and adhered to comply with the audit recommendation.

We have recommended that in order to validate the accuracy, existence and completeness of the properties of the municipality as well as to establish the correct valuation, sufficient control and accountability for each property, the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the following:
The members of the Inventory Committee to conduct the annual physical inventory, prepare the RPCPPE and/or may seek assistance or coordinate with the department heads to make inventory of municipal properties in their respective offices, so as to address the manpower constraint in the conduct of physical count.
The Property Officer/GSO and Municipal Accountant to update the property/supply ledger cards and subsidiary ledgers, respectively, reconcile the same, and make the necessary adjustments. Additional staff to address the concern on manpower constraints may also be considered.

We have recommended that Management direct:
The Inventory Committee to:
Conduct an inventory of the local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipality; and
Render a Report on the Physical count of the Road Networks System.
The Municipal General Service Officer to:
Render a Report on Local Road Network and Road Map;
Regularly maintain and update the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Service Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
The Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare the JEV to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components;
Prepare and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card; and
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network.

We have recommended that the Management:
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the General Services Officer and Municipal Assessor to exert further efforts in retrieval of documents required to establish ownership and cost valuation of the unrecorded land to facilitate its recording in the books. at acquisition cost, or fair value, if acquired through non-exchange transaction;
Make the necessary adjustment by recording the land account at acquisition cost, or fair value, if acquired through non-exchange transaction, based on available supporting documents; and
Initiate the transfer of ownership of road lots to various barangays which are not held for use by the Municipal Government.

We have recommended Management to:
Cause the Municipal Treasurer to update and maintain the RPTARs as primary basis of duly certified list of taxpayers for the corresponding amount of tax collectible as a basis for setting up the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year. Also, verify and ascertain the list of delinquent taxpayers to its actual collectability; and
Cause the Municipal Accountant to only consider the amount of duly certified list from the Treasury office which reconciles with the amount of collectibles based from the assessment roll of the Assessor’s Office. Make necessary adjustment, if any, to present fairly the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact and daily or on the next banking day.  We have further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of temporarily using the collection to pay petty expenses.

We have recommended that Management, through the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, enforce strictly the provisions cited in Section 272 of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160 and Section 86 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the close monitoring and recording of SET collections.

We have recommended that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board allocate the SEF Budget only for eligible and priority programs, projects and activities allowed by the law and encourage schools to make use of the Department of Education’s funds for their regular operations.
We have further recommended that Management thru the Municipal Local School Board ensure that funds allocated for Special Education Fund be budgeted and utilized strictly in accordance with R.A. No. 7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 in order to attain the intended purpose for which the fund was created.

We have recommended that Management:
Adhere to the principles of Imprest Fund System in maintaining the Petty Cash Fund set forth under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and
Refrain from using the Petty Cash Fund for the payment of regular expenditures such as, internet expenses, office supplies, traveling and gasoline expenses which could be paid out of other regular disbursement process.

We have recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer reflect in the RCD the gross amount and the discounts of RPT Collections and not net of discounts to enable the Municipal Accountant to account for discount granted on prompt payment of real property taxes using the above accounts as provided for under IPSAS so that the correct balances of income, assets and liabilities will be reflected in the financial statements.
We have recommended further that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following journal entry to recognize discounts of RPT collections for fair presentation of the financial statements, to wit:

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
Municipal Engineer to notify the contractors about the defects and require them to perform corrective actions to prolong the life of LGU’s infrastructure projects;
Project Monitoring Committee to perform regular and thorough monitoring and supervision of projects to ensure that only those satisfactorily completed and in accordance with the specifications are paid; and
Municipal Engineer to conduct post-evaluation of completed projects before expiration of the warranty period in order to determine project conditions and possible actions that may be taken to protect the interest of the LGU.

We have recommended Management to:
require the MDRRMC to monitor and evaluate the implementation of projects/programs as planned;
direct the MDRRMC to convene in order to review and reprogram unutilized QRF and DRRMF – MOOE for the approval of the Municipal Development Council; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to revert back the amount of ₱508,465.02 CY 2014 Special Trust Fund to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund which be made available for other social services of the Municipality.

We have recommended that management stop from charging PPAs which are not directly related to disaster risk reduction and management activities to the LDRRM Fund and the Municipal Accounting and Budget Offices prepare the necessary adjustments of these expenditures in the books and to strictly comply with the provisions of RA 10121, COA Circular No. 2012-002 and JMC No. 2013-1.

We have recommended Management to:
Require additional payment for the uncollected penalties during the CY 2019. Thereafter, strictly implement with the penalty provisions of the current Tax Ordinance, requiring assessment and collection of penalties for delayed payments of business taxes as additional income or funds for projects to be implemented as well as ensure earlier collection of the said funds; and
Require for the certified copy of Annual Income Tax Return to Business Taxpayers on the second quarter of the year and adjust the business tax and collect the difference if there are discrepancies between the amount appearing in the ITR and gross sales declared in the business tax application form.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to make a representation with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office to seek assistance in the enforcement of collection of delinquent taxes, especially those long over-due.  Moreover, to ask assistance from the Barangay Officials for them to inform the heirs of the deceased delinquent taxpayers or occupants of the properties to pay their delinquent taxes.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive the following:
Revisit the organization structure of the municipality so as to create positions which are essential in the delivery of public service to the constituents and to lessen the number of JOCs,
Submit/attach complete Accomplishment Reports and certification that the activities/services cannot be provided by regular and permanent personnel to prove the validity and legality of the disbursements pertaining to their salaries,
Submit the basic documentary requirements to support the payment of wages and overtime pay to job order personnel, and
Strictly comply with the abovementioned provisions on the hiring of JOCs.

We have recommended that Management ensure that Budget and Accounting Office strictly comply with the provision of RA 9679 in observing the maximum limit of ₱100.00 per month per official/employee.
Further, we have also recommended that Management make representation with the HDMF La Union Regional Office to offset the excess contribution as against the future remittances of the LGU’s share. Otherwise, concerned employees/officials refund the relative amount due to the LGU as these were already applied as part of their provident savings and future claims from the HDMF.

We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments made in accordance with the Revised Withholding Tax Table and the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period. Further, we have recommended that management submit the required necessary supporting documents to support the payment of BAC Honoraria to the BAC Members, TWG and BAC Secretariat and comply with Section 5.7.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We have recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report and approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We have recommended that management adopt measures that would facilitate the submission of their accounts on time as prescribed in the foregoing Sections of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and P.D. 1445. We have further recommended that additional staff with accounting background be assigned at the Accounting Office in order to address the delay on the submission of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management: 

Limit the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the GAA and ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed take home pay which is ₱5,000.00 as provided under Section 48 of Republic Act 10964.

Ensure that the required minimum take home pay is attained after all the mandatory and other authorized deductions from the salary of each employee are deducted from the gross salary to guarantee that there is enough amount to sustain the needs of the employee for a month.

 See to it that an employee applying for a loan will only avail of the amount that has a monthly amortization of not more than the amount in excess of ₱5,000.00 after deducting all the authorized deductions.
observations.managementAction:
The management advised the accountable officers to discontinue the practice of allowing deductions on monthly loan amortizations of private lending institutions from the personnel’s payrolls other than those mandated by law. 

Those personnel who contracted loans from these lending establishments will now directly remit their payments to the collecting agents/office of these creditors and to ensure that each employee receives a net take home pay of at least P 5, 000.00 for CY 2019 pursuant to the General Appropriations Acts of 2018, we issued HR Memorandum No. 08, series of 2019 incorporating your recommendations.
observations.reason: As a result of post audit of monthly payrolls by the Audit Team, the monthly net take home pay of employees does not fall under the mandated minimum amount of ₱5,000.00.

Otherwise, administrative disciplinary action might be imposed and/or filing of criminal charges against the Municipal Treasurer and concerned Accountable Officers.
The Municipal Accountant to properly take-up the collections, remittances and deposits in the books of accounts.
We have also recommended strict compliance to the aforementioned provisions of NGAS Manual, P.D. 1445 Revised Cash Examination Manual to prevent the possible misuse or misappropriation of public funds.

We have recommended that Management cause the Municipal Treasurer to perform religiously the verification process in order to ensure prompt remittance/turnover and recording of collections for a particular day and to implement strictly the provisions of Sections 30 and 32 of the NGAS for LGUs, that is, collectors remit daily to the Municipal Treasurer so that the collections be deposited daily.

We have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Health Office to properly dispose the expired medicines in order to avoid erroneous and unintentional distribution to the constituents.  Likewise, medicines which are about to expire be distributed immediately to the people in need so that the health service of the Municipality will not be imperiled and that government funds used for the procurement will not be wasted. We have recommended further that the Bids and Awards Committee include in the Invitation to Bid the adoption of one time bidding but staggered delivery as the case maybe, to prevent overstocking of medicines. Also, the Inventory Committee strictly monitor the expiry dates of procured medicines to prevent expired medicines resulting to wastage of government funds. We have further recommended that the Accounting Office make the necessary adjustments for its fair presentation in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer reflect in the RCD the gross amount and the discounts of RPT Collections and not net of discounts to enable the OIC-Municipal Accountant to account for discount granted on prompt payment of real property taxes using the above accounts as provided for under NGAS and PPSAS so that the correct balances of income, assets and liabilities will be reflected in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity instruct the Procurement Officer to purchase common-use supplies from the DBM-PS and resort to the use of the alternative modes of procurement only when the needed supplies are not available with the DBM-PS as supported by a certification by the said Office.

We have recommended that immediate installation of adequate controls for requisition of gasoline and other oil derivatives must be made through enforcement of necessary documents as required by existing rules and regulations such as:
Corresponding driver’s trip tickets and monthly report of official travels to support the use of government vehicles must be attached in the Disbursement Vouchers for payment of gasoline and oil derivative.
The supply officer must prepare reconciliation statement between the detailed schedule of gasoline issued by the gasoline station / dealer and the supply officer’s record. Duplicate of requisition slip must be kept on file by the Supply Officer as one of his basis in preparing reconciliation statement.
A written agreement or contract with the gasoline dealer must be undertaken to formalize the terms and conditions as well as limitations/restrictions.
We have recommended further that officials concerned should strictly comply with the provisions of Section 9.1.3 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on all government transactions/disbursements. Likewise we have further recommended that these transactions/disbursements should be subjected to tax as mandated under Revenue Memorandum No. 23-2012 dated May 8, 2012 reiterating the responsibilities of the officials and employees of government offices for the withholding of applicable taxes on certain income payments and the imposition of penalties for non - compliance thereof.

We have recommended management, thru the BAC, that purchases of various supplies and materials be made thru the general mode of procurement which is public bidding to do away with the practice of reimbursement. If competitive bidding cannot be ultimately pursued, the BAC through a resolution, shall justify and recommend the change on the method of procurement to be approved by the Local Chief Executive. Strict adherence to the provision under COA Circular No. 97-002 regarding the Imprest Fund System in order to have a more efficient and effective internal control is also enjoined.

We have recommended that Management:
a. Limit the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the GAA and ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed take home pay which is ₱5,000.00 as provided under Section 48 of Republic Act 10964.
b. Ensure that the required minimum take home pay is attained after all the mandatory and other authorized deductions from the salary of each employee are deducted from the gross salary to guarantee that there is enough amount to sustain the needs of the employee for a month.
c. See to it that an employee applying for a loan will only avail of the amount that has a monthly amortization of not more than the amount in excess of ₱5,000.00 after deducting all the authorized deductions.

We have recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant:
1. Regularly prepare Monthly Report on the Utilization of the DRRMF which shall be submitted by the LDRRMO on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor; and
2. Ensure that at the start of every year, all unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of previous years are transferred to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRMF” in accordance with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We have recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report and approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We have recommended further that the members of the BAC and BAC Secretariat especially closely coordinate with each other and ensure completeness of procurement documents to effectively facilitate submission to COA.

We have recommended that management adopt measures that would facilitate the submission of their accounts on time as prescribed in the foregoing Sections of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and P.D. 1445. We have further recommended that additional staff with accounting background be assigned at the Accounting Office in order to address the delay on the submission of accounts.

We have recommended that management designate permanent employees as collecting officers in order they be properly bonded in compliance with the said Treasury Circular. We have further recommended that no accountable officer be made to continue to perform his duties unless bonded.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere strictly with the provision of Sec. 31 of Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs and prepare and submit the original copies of JEV together with RCD and other supporting source documents.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer properly maintain the Cashbooks, reconcile it with the accounting records and to record cash transactions therein in accordance with Sections 26 to 28 of Vol. II MNGAS for LGUs in order to monitor balances at any given time.

We have recommended that the MT prepare and submit regularly the monthly Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms in order to establish the accountability of accountable forms at any given time.
