observations.recommendation:
For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we recommended that
management conduct the required physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every
year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to
be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical
existence of the fixed assets and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify
unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records
is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pinpoint
responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs. Moreover, we further
recommended that Management maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger
cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper
control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them. For control and
monitoring, make use of ICS for semi-expendable items to avoid loss, as these are usually
small items.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Inventory
Committee to include in the conduct of inventory of the properties of the
municipality the infrastructures and other community facilities. We recommended
further that a) the Inventory Committee prepare and submit the required Report to
the DILG; and b) the Municipal Accountant complete the Registry of Public
Infrastructure to facilitate recognition in the book of accounts and disclosure in the
Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that management require the OIC - Municipal
Accountant to prepare the following journal entry to recognize completed projects
under BUB and ADM as assets in the books of the agency in compliance with
PPSAS Nos. 1 and 17 and DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-3
dated May 23, 2017 for fair presentation of the financial statements, to wit:

Road Network 26,808,664.41
Building 3,889,514.59
Water System 17,864,874.43
Government Equity 48,563,053.43

We recommended that Management cause the Municipal Treasurer to
perform religiously the verification process in order to ensure prompt
remittance/turnover and recording of collections for a particular day and to
implement strictly the provisions of Sections 30 and 32 of the NGAS for LGUs, that
is, collectors remit daily to the Municipal Treasurer so that the collections be
deposited daily.

We recommended that management designate permanent employees as
collecting officers in order they be properly bonded in compliance with the said
Treasury Circular. We further recommend that no accountable officer be made to
continue to perform his duties unless bonded.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive the following:
1. Revisit the organization structure of the municipality so as to create
positions which are essential in the delivery of public service to the
constituents and to lessen the number of JOCs,
2. Submit/attach Accomplishment Reports and certification that the
activities/services cannot be provided by regular and permanent
personnel to prove the validity and legality of the disbursements
pertaining to their salaries.
3. Strictly comply with the abovementioned provisions on the hiring of
JOCs.

We recommended that officials concerned should strictly comply with the
provisions of Section 9.1.3 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on all
government transactions/disbursements. Likewise, we further recommended that
these transactions/ disbursements should be subjected to tax as mandated under
Revenue Memorandum No. 23-2012 dated May 8, 2012 reiterating the
responsibilities of the officials and employees of government offices for the
withholding of applicable taxes on certain income payments and the imposition of
penalties for non - compliance thereof.

We recommended that management strengthen control over procurement
activities by:
1) Checks should be drawn in the name of the supplier
2) Require the accountant to withhold taxes due to the government
3) Limit reimbursement payment, particularly in times of emergency
4) Procurement should be made base on APP schedule to avoid out of stock
situation

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following, that prior to the
implementation of the project, proper planning, study and assessment should be made
to see to it that its goal and objective on the proposed project will be achieved
efficiently and effectively. Furthermore, to look into the possible viability to
repair/fix the project rather than it being left idle, unattended and exposed to
elements causing it to deteriorate.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Procurement
Officer to purchase common-use supplies from the DBM-PS and resort to the use of
the alternative modes of procurement only when the needed supplies are not
available with the DBM-PS as supported by a certification by the said Office.

We recommended that Management require the BAC Chairman/Secretariat
to submit copies of awarded contracts, irrespective of amount and all related
documents forming part thereof to COA within five (5) days upon perfection/
issuance. Further, instruct the Chairman of the Inspection and Acceptance
Committee to notify this Office within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries of
procured items.

We recommended immediate submission of unsubmitted accounts. We
further recommend close monitoring and coordination between the
Accounting office and the Treasurer's office to facilitate timely submission of
mandatory reports and/or accounts as prescribed by the regulations.

Relative to the foregoing, we recommended management to organize the
LDRRMO in accordance with the provisions under RA 10121 and its IRR. We
recommended further that the LDRRMO provide and design specific programs,
projects and activities that are responsive to disaster risk reduction in order to
optimize utilization of LDRRM Fund, monitor on its implementation and report on
its result.

We recommended Management to appropriate in the Annual Budget at least
one percent (1%) of their IRA for LCPC programs, projects and activities as
required under Section 15 of RA 9344. We recommend further that the design of
PPAs should be in accordance with the enumeration under Section II of DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we recommended that management conduct the required physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed assets and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pinpoint responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs. Moreover, we further recommended that Management maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them. For control and monitoring, make use of ICS for semi-expendable items to avoid loss, as these are usually small items.

the attainment of the benefits of the disaster mitigation, prevention and
preparedness. We recommended further that Management optimize the utilization
of LDRRM Fund, monitor on its implementation and report on its result as
required under the provisions of the above sited laws rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of PCW-
DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and Part V of COA Circular No. 2004-001
and institutionalize the creation of a GFPS who will take a lead in mainstreaming
gender in agency PAPs, coordinate the preparation of the agency's GAD AR,
monitor its implementation and report on its results.

We have recommended that the Inventory Committee submit a properly accomplished RPCPPE per department and sections. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments and to pinpoint responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs. The GSO/Property Custodian to maintain complete property ledger cards and stock card/property cards and reconcile with the reported balance in the Financial Statements not later than January 31 of each year to avoid accumulation of unreconciled balance of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: A physical inventory conducted by the Inventory Committee per department and sections. Physical Inventory Report was made but there are still some costs of prior year's PPEs that cannot be determined due to non-availability of documents as basis for cost.
observations.reason: Some costs of prior year's PPEs cannot be determined due to non-availability of documents as basis for cost.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman/Secretariat submit copies of awarded contracts, irrespective of amount and all related documents forming part thereof to COA within five (5) days upon perfection/issuance. Further, instruct the Chairman of the Inspection and Acceptance Committee to notify this Office within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries of procured items.
observations.managementAction: Management advised that the BAC secretariat adhere with the recommendations. However, due to monthly limited budget on reports, the Municipality only submits the completed/awarded contract and Purchase Orders at the end of the month. The completed/awarded contract documents were not submitted with the monthly reports nor within five days from perfection.
observations.reason: Completed/awarded contract documents were not submitted with the monthly reports nor within five days from perfection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended immediate submission of unsubmitted accounts. We further recommended close monitoring and coordination between the Accounting office and the Treasurer's office to facilitate timely submission of mandatory reports and/or accounts as prescribed by the regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management confidently assured the Team the early submission of the mandatory reports and/or accounts. If hiring of additional staff is not likely, we have recommended close monitoring and coordination between the Accounting office and the Treasurer's office to facilitate timely submission of accounts.
observations.reason: Monthly Reports were still delayed for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the OIC-Accountant create a Special Trust Fund account solely for disaster risk reduction and the transfer of unexpended DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer. We have recommended further that the OIC-Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for transfers of unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer.
observations.managementAction: A Special Trust Fund Account was opened at LBP, San Fernando City solely for the transfer of unexpended DRRRM Fund. However, only amount of P475,835.91 was deposited in the account.
observations.reason: Only P475,835.91 was deposited in the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to comply strictly with the provisions of RA9184. The Bids and Awards Committee, upon approval of the Chief Executive of the Consolidated Procurement Plan, should follow strictly the provisions of RA 9184. Management should discontinue the reimbursement basis as mode for paying procured items or services and strictly adhere to the requirements that all the payments must be made by check pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated Feb. 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: Management is already deducting the 10% retention money on infra projects and all its procurements; sometimes supplies are procured through emergency purchase so as not to delay the process of office transactions.
observations.reason: Supplies were sometimes procured through emergency purchase.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipality to designate clearly the responsibility for the registration of motor vehicles and thereafter require the concerned official liable to refund the amount of penalties paid. We recommend further that the Municipality strictly implement the provisions of Section 4.7 of the above Joint Circular to avoid payment of unnecessary expenses.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, one motor vehicle that is undergoing repair was not brought for registration. The municipal Assessor office is still in the process of reconciling her records with the accounting records.
observations.reason: One motor vehicle that is undergoing repair was not brought for registration.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) and Taxpayers Index Cards, and to expedite the reconciliation of her records with the accounting records as well as the records of the Municipal/Provincial Assessor, for an accurate basis for the setting up of the RPT/SET Receivables accounts at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: Accounting office has already coordinated with the office of the Municipal Assessor for data regarding the actual collectibles based on the RPTAR. The Municipal Assessor office is still in the process of reconciling her records with the Accounting records as well as the records of the Municipal/Provincial Assessor, for an accurate basis for the setting up of the RPT/SET Receivable accounts at the beginning of the year.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor office is still in the process of reconciling her records with the Accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require all Deputized Collecting Officers and the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all their collections as well as all collections turned over to him by the collectors/tellers with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day; and to record all deposits made in the cashbook and prepare regularly the Report of Collections and Deposits in accordance to Section 32 of the NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal treasurer has already issued a Memorandum to all her collectors to strictly remit their collections to the Liquidating Officer and for the Liquidating Officer to deposit intact the daily collection to the depository bank.
observations.reason: These were being deposited the next banking day.
